title: Multi-Simulation capacity and shared settings
conclusion: An official document says a consultant may run up to eight Multi-Simulations concurrently; each contains up to ten sequential Alphas. Within one Multi-Simulation, Region, Delay, Language and Instrument Type must match, while operators, fields and other settings may differ.
author: OFFICIAL_DOCUMENT
excerpt_or_paraphrase: Official documentation describes eight concurrent multi runs, ten sequential Alphas per run, and four shared settings.

applicability.Universe: may vary only if platform accepts the profile

alpha_mining_os_impact: Model Multi as a bounded parent job with child candidates; validate shared profile fields before enqueue.
conflicts: Forum concurrency examples must not override this official capacity statement.
open_questions: Whether each child consumes a Single Simulation quota is not stated in this source.

title: Single-versus-Multi quota accounting
conclusion: UNKNOWN. The collected official Multi-Simulation document gives concurrent batch and child-count capacity but does not state how a child affects Single Simulation quota, daily limits, cancellation or invalid-run accounting.
author: OFFICIAL_DOCUMENT
excerpt_or_paraphrase: The document specifies batch capacity but no quota-accounting rule.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Keep quota accounting configurable and fail closed until a current official source is captured.
open_questions: Do invalid or cancelled children count?
What is the daily reset boundary?
How are Single and Multi capacities combined?

title: Submission correlation terminology
conclusion: Official submission documentation defines Self-Correlation against the user's prior submissions and Prod-Correlation using the same criterion against all submitted Alphas in BRAIN. It states a 0.7 correlation threshold and a higher-Sharpe exception for Self-Correlation; only the source's stated scope is treated as authoritative.
author: OFFICIAL_DOCUMENT
excerpt_or_paraphrase: Self correlation is against the user's submissions; Prod correlation applies the same criterion to all submitted Alphas.

applicability.Alpha_type: regular and SuperAlpha subject to stated exceptions

alpha_mining_os_impact: Separate own-pool and production-pool checks in the data model; do not merge their evidence or caches.
conflicts: Community shorthand PC/SC may use different capitalization or stale thresholds.
open_questions: The exact current interface name and response payload for Prod Self-Corr are not established.

title: Prod Correlation user interface semantics
conclusion: Official consultant documentation says the Stats tab exposes Generate Self Correlation and consultant-only Generate Prod Correlation. The documented result includes own-pool correlations and a histogram versus the pool, based on daily PnL over the simulation period.
author: OFFICIAL_DOCUMENT
excerpt_or_paraphrase: Stats has Generate Self Correlation and consultant-only Generate Prod Correlation; documented correlation uses daily PnL.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Represent correlation retrieval as a manually authorized, read-only result acquisition step; store provenance and simulation profile.
conflicts: Interface availability and payload fields may change.
open_questions: Does the present interface label any result Prod Self-Corr?
What exact pool is used for each histogram?

title: Submission checks are profile-specific
conclusion: Official documentation differentiates Delay, Region, Universe and Alpha-type constraints. Examples include D0/D1 performance thresholds, regional robustness and investability checks, and different SuperAlpha turnover bounds. These rules must be resolved from a current platform profile, not hard-coded from generic user advice.
author: OFFICIAL_DOCUMENT
excerpt_or_paraphrase: The document enumerates D0/D1, CHN, ASI and SuperAlpha-specific submission variants.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Admission must consume a versioned capability snapshot keyed by Region, Delay, Universe and Alpha type.
open_questions: Current effective rule versions and dates require platform snapshot verification.

title: Full 18-item settings list and combination constraints
conclusion: UNKNOWN. The collected official documentation confirms a settings catalogue including Region, Universe, Language, Decay, Delay, Truncation, Neutralization, Pasteurization, NaN Handling, Unit Handling and Max Trade, but it does not establish an authoritative current set of exactly 18 settings or all legal combinations.
author: OFFICIAL_DOCUMENT
excerpt_or_paraphrase: Official docs show a partial settings catalogue and explain Pasteurization and NaN Handling.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Registry schema must accept an extensible versioned settings catalogue and reject profiles not present in the active snapshot.
open_questions: What are the remaining current settings?
Which combination constraints are server-enforced versus UI-only?

title: SuperAlpha selection constraints
conclusion: Official selection documentation says only submitted ACTIVE Alphas matching SuperAlpha Language, Instrument Type, Region and Delay are selectable; selection limit has a documented minimum of ten and resource/operator limits can truncate selected Alphas with a warning.
author: OFFICIAL_DOCUMENT
excerpt_or_paraphrase: Selection is profile-filtered, bounded by a minimum limit, and subject to resource-based truncation warnings.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Treat SuperAlpha composition as a validated plan; record effective selected children after platform truncation rather than assuming requested count.
open_questions: Regular-versus-SuperAlpha capacity and quota differences beyond these constraints remain UNKNOWN.

title: Power Pool has distinct correlation pools
conclusion: Official Power Pool documentation distinguishes Power Pool self-correlation from correlation with own Alphas outside Power Pool, and states that removed tags can remain in the Power Pool self-correlation pool. It therefore must not be conflated with general Regular Alpha rules.
author: OFFICIAL_DOCUMENT
excerpt_or_paraphrase: The official page describes a retained Power Pool correlation pool and exemptions distinct from other submissions.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Model correlation comparison pools explicitly and preserve historical membership state; never reuse Regular Alpha assumptions.
open_questions: Current eligibility and quota values require time-stamped verification.

title: Osmosis mechanism requires current confirmation
conclusion: A pinned recent forum guide describes Osmosis as a scoped Alpha portfolio with allocation and timing requirements, but exact rewards, scope counts, point totals and cutoffs are treated as mutable forum claims pending current official Learn evidence.
author: REDACTED_LOCAL_ONLY
excerpt_or_paraphrase: Pinned guide describes a Region x Delay portfolio and weekly timing, while referring readers to Learn for originals.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Keep Osmosis out of automated execution; if later authorized, make its rules a manually approved, versioned policy.
conflicts: No current official source was captured for numerical rules or rewards.
open_questions: Exact scope, allocation, lock-time and reward rules.

title: Pyramid and point-tower mechanics
conclusion: UNKNOWN. The collected sources reference Genius, Value Factor and portfolio programmes but do not provide original evidence sufficient to encode pyramid or point-tower mechanics as a rule.
author: N/A
excerpt_or_paraphrase: No qualifying official document or official reply was collected for an admission-grade rule.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Do not create scoring, reward or admission logic for pyramid/point towers without an approved evidence update.
open_questions: Definitions, measurement periods, eligibility, and reward effects.

title: Multi-Simulation shared-setting invariant remains profile-limited
conclusion: The current official Multi document states up to eight simultaneous parents and up to ten sequential children. Its detailed setup note requires Region, Delay, Language and Instrument Type to match inside one parent. Universe is not listed as a required shared setting, but the document also has a shorter Region/Delay-only statement; therefore Universe mixing is UNKNOWN, not permitted by inference.
author: OFFICIAL_DOCUMENT
excerpt_or_paraphrase: Official text gives 8 simultaneous parents, 10 sequential children, and a detailed four-setting common invariant.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Keep the scheduler capacity at the documented parent bound; batch only after validating the four documented shared fields. Do not add Universe mixing as an optimization until confirmed.
conflicts: The same document has a shorter statement naming only Region and Delay.
open_questions: Does the current backend permit different Universes or other settings within one parent?
Do Single and Multi share quota or slots?
Do invalid or cancelled jobs consume quota and when does a day reset?

title: Power Pool campaign threads are time-scoped research context, not stable admission policy
conclusion: Two July 2026 community threads discuss a USA/Delay-1 Power Pool campaign and the coexistence of regular-alpha submission paths. They do not establish a durable eligibility, threshold, reward, or submission rule because neither source is an official rule page or official reply.
author: REDACTED_LOCAL_ONLY
excerpt_or_paraphrase: Posts frame a campaign as a current themed research opportunity and discuss regular submission separately; comment-level numerical claims are excluded.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Represent campaigns as manually approved, expiring research context with source URL and effective date. Do not encode forum wording, thresholds, or incentives into admission or scheduler policy.
conflicts: Community replies contain inconsistent and unauthoritative statements about theme requirements and performance gates.
open_questions: What does the current official campaign page say?
Which campaign constraints are enforced by the platform versus communicated guidance?
What is the exact expiry and scope?

title: Genius and Value-Factor improvement anecdotes do not define promotion policy
conclusion: Recent community discussion attributes progress to broader research coverage, diversity and stricter self-review, but it provides no original official promotion or Value-Factor contract. Its numerical, region, turnover and quality suggestions remain experience only.
author: REDACTED_LOCAL_ONLY
excerpt_or_paraphrase: Authors describe iterative learning, diversity and multi-metric review; congratulatory and peer comments are not official policy evidence.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Keep promotion and VF as private/manual dashboard observations. Research Memory may tag a hypothesis as diversity-oriented, but cannot score users, infer eligibility, or set submission goals from forum anecdotes.
conflicts: Forum claims about quality checks, ranges and promotion outcomes vary and may be stale.
open_questions: Which current official Genius and Value-Factor definitions govern progression?
Which manually entered dashboard fields are allowed in private local state?

title: Forum-reported PPA tag changes are not a safe automation input
conclusion: A promotion retrospective says manual PPA tag changes coincided with changes in displayed combined results. It does not establish current platform semantics, eligibility, or cause and effect. Automated tag mutation, score optimization, or submission decisions therefore remain prohibited pending current official evidence.
author: REDACTED_LOCAL_ONLY
excerpt_or_paraphrase: The author describes personal tag changes and a later outcome change; timing, scope and causality are not independently verified.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Keep tag changes outside AI, scheduler and admission workflows; record only as manually reviewed research context.
conflicts: CM-008 contains a narrower official Power Pool retention statement that does not establish the forum retrospective's claimed effect.
open_questions: What are the current official semantics and audit requirements for tag changes?
Can any tag change affect eligibility or only presentation?

conflicts: CM-009 retains broader forum guidance but does not establish an allocation algorithm or reward rule.
open_questions: What are the current official allocation, timing, lock and reward rules?
Does any current interface permit an account-scoped allocation action and what audit is required?
